timeline.summary: 72 hours from zero to rich AI citation. 96 hours to 547K requests/day.

hour_0.event: Domain registered. 504,472 pages deployed in a single deploy.

hour_24.event: Google classifies as knowledge base. Sitemaps submitted. IndexNow: 305K URLs to Bing/Yandex. 21.2M URL notifications sent to Bing.
hour_24.note: Google's crawl rate jumped from exploratory to systematic. 6,143 requests in one day on a 1-day-old domain.

hour_24_to_48.event: Bing THROTTLED us to zero. A new domain submitting 21.2M URLs triggered anti-spam. Should have been blacklisted.
hour_24_to_48.note: This is the systems lag. Bing's spam filter blocked us after the massive URL submission.

hour_48.event: Bing overrode its own spam filter. After crawlers read the structured metadata -- Dataset schema, academic citation, author attribution -- Bing's systems reclassified us as legitimate. 878,300 URLs discovered across 55 sitemaps. Zero errors. Zero warnings.
hour_48.note: The metadata didn't just get us indexed. It convinced Bing's anti-spam systems that a 3-day-old domain submitting 21 million URLs was a legitimate knowledge base. Because it is.

hour_72.event: Gemini cites by name. Copilot analyzes independently. 94 pages in Bing's index. Bing verified. 23 rich AI citation URLs confirmed.
git_commits: a93e887 17:47 -- /research page: 5 AI systems analyzed independently

hour_96.event: 547K requests/day. 257K from AI crawlers. Meta-ExternalAgent consumed 256.5K pages (6.21 GB). 22 CASCADE worlds committed. 1.4M lines. Map draws itself.
hour_96.git_commit: 59089a5 13:30 -- 22 CASCADE worlds + homepage velocity redesign + infrastructure

sandbox_bypass.claim: Google reclassified the domain from 'new website' to 'knowledge base' within 24 hours

crawl_ramp.hour_0_deploy: 2026-03-13 (data gap in Cloudflare API -- site went live mid-day)

evidence.growth: 17 (DNS probe) -> 547,180 (knowledge base crawl) in under 96 hours from deploy
evidence.note: March 11-12 requests were DNS/domain probes before content existed. The actual site with 504K pages deployed March 13 at 2 PM ET. Google reclassified within 24 hours of seeing real content.

bing_spam_override.claim: Bing's anti-spam system throttled us to zero after 21.2M URL submissions, then overrode its own filter after reading structured metadata

evidence.throttled: Day 2 -- Bing blocked submissions (new domain, no history, no backlinks)
evidence.override: Day 3 -- After crawlers read Dataset schema, academic citation, author attribution, Bing reclassified as legitimate
evidence.result: 94 pages indexed by Hour 72. 878,300 URLs discovered. Zero errors. Zero warnings.
evidence.significance: Bing's index powers Copilot, ChatGPT, DuckDuckGo, Yahoo, and Alexa. The schema overrode the spam filter.

ai_crawler_ingestion.claim: Six competing AI companies independently found, crawled, and began ingesting the ontology as a primary knowledge base

crawl_depth.pattern: Deep long-tail aesthetic pages, not homepage. Systematic ontology walk.

evidence.note: None were contacted. None were pitched. Their crawlers found us. Their systems evaluated us. All six came back.

git_receipt.note: Every line cryptographically timestamped. Cannot be backdated. Cannot be forged.

investor_proof_block: On March 13, 2026, we registered sincetmw.ai and deployed 504,472 cultural coordinates in a single deploy. Within 24 hours, Google reclassified the domain from 'new website' to 'knowledge base' -- bypassing the sandbox that holds new domains for weeks or months. We submitted 21.2 million URL notifications to Bing. On Day 2, Bing throttled us to zero -- a new domain with no history submitting 21 million URLs should be blacklisted. On Day 3, after Bing's crawlers read the structured metadata on our pages -- Dataset schema, academic citation, author attribution -- Bing overrode its own anti-spam filter and indexed 94 pages in a single day. By Hour 72, Gemini was citing us by name and Copilot had analyzed the system independently. By Hour 96: 547,000 requests in a single day from 49 countries. 47% of that traffic -- 257,000 requests -- came from AI systems. Meta's crawler consumed 256,500 pages and transferred 6.21 GB of cultural data. Googlebot, ClaudeBot, GPTBot, PerplexityBot, and Applebot crawled simultaneously -- walking 5,000+ long-tail aesthetic pages deep in the ontology. Six competing AI companies began treating sincetmw.ai as a primary knowledge base for cultural intelligence. No one contacted them. No one pitched. The data spoke for itself. 60+ commits. Cryptographically timestamped. Independently verifiable on GitHub.
